channel.title: fractions on Noam Zaks
channel.description: Recent content in fractions on Noam Zaks

item.title: Adjacent Fractions
item.description: We look at all of the rational numbers in $ (0, 1] $ whose denominator is smaller than some $ n \in \mathbb N $. Prove that for any two adjacent numbers $ \frac {p_i} {q_i}, \frac {p_{i+1}} {q_{i+1}} $ the following holds: $ p_{i+1} q_i - p_i q_{i+1} = 1 $. Here’s an example for $ n = 4 $: Here’s another (with the same code) for $ n = 7 $:

item.title: Segment Splitting
item.description:
Given a set of pairwise-disjoint intervals (such that the intersection of every pair is $ \emptyset $) in $ [0, 1] $, assuming the total length is greater than $ \frac 1 2 $, show there are two points $ a,b $ in the union of the segments such that $ |a-b| = \frac 1 {10} $. Here are a few examples
from manim import * class SegmentSplittingExample(Scene): min_width = .
